title: NDMA issues heatwave alert from April 11 in Sindh and Punjab - minutemirror.com.pk
full_text:
Summary
- Islamabad: An alert has been issued across various parts of the country in view of a heatwave threat starting from April 11.
- According to details, the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a heatwave alert for southern districts of Sindh and Punjab starting April 11.
- In Punjab, a heatwave alert has been issued for Bahawalnagar, Bahawalpur and Rahim Yar Khan due to increasing heat intensity, while heatwave conditions are also expected in Jhal Magsi, Usta Muhammad and Dera Murad Jamali.
Islamabad: An alert has been issued across various parts of the country in view of a heatwave threat starting from April 11. According to details, the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued a heatwave alert for southern districts of Sindh and Punjab starting April 11.
The NDMA stated that an unusual rise in temperature is expected in the coming days which may remain several degrees above normal. The alert further stated that daytime temperatures may remain 5 to 7 degrees above normal while nighttime temperatures could be 3 to 5 degrees above normal.
Sindh: Districts including Dadu, Jacobabad, Larkana and surrounding areas will remain under intense heat. In addition, heatwave conditions are also likely in Jamshoro, Qambar Shahdadkot, Ghotki, Khairpur, Sanghar, Umerkot and Kashmore.
In Punjab, a heatwave alert has been issued for Bahawalnagar, Bahawalpur and Rahim Yar Khan due to increasing heat intensity, while heatwave conditions are also expected in Jhal Magsi, Usta Muhammad and Dera Murad Jamali.
The NDMA has appealed to the public to drink plenty of water to avoid dehydration, avoid unnecessary outdoor movement during daytime especially in the afternoon and wear light-coloured cotton clothing when going outside. People are also advised to keep their heads covered.
The NDMA has also instructed the relevant provincial and district administrations to remain alert so that hospital arrangements are fully prepared to handle any emergency situations or cases of heatstroke.
